statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat:
The input will be provided from stdin in the following format: n 
arr[i]
statement.outputFormat: Output the minimum cost to climb n stairs.
statement.notes: Note that the cost to climb one stair is always 1, and the cost to climb two stairs is provided as arr[i].

constraints.description: Number of stairs with given cost

examples.explanation: The minimum cost is 3 if we start from the first stair and go 1,2 or from the second stair and go 1 from there.

hints.text: Correctly initialize your dp array and make sure you handle edge cases properly.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: Dynamic Programming is used to solve this problem because the problem has overlapping subproblems.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force is not suitable for this problem because there are too many possible combinations to try.
editorial.betterSolution: The better solution is a bottom-up Dynamic Programming approach.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a bottom-up Dynamic Programming approach with memoization.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by analyzing its time and space complexities.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the solution would involve writing an example input and output.
